ocr.text: In recent years, the implementation of advanced monitoring technologies has become essential for managing water resources
effectively, particularly in regions prone to flooding or drought. Nakhon Si Thammarat, Thailand, has adopted innovative
solutions to enhance its water management capabilities through the integration of the STARLFLOW Ultrasonic Doppler sensor
system. This system provides real-time data on critical water parameters, specifically water level and velocity, which are crucial
for assessing hydrological conditions. The data collected through these measurements enables local authorities to make
informed decisions regarding water management and disaster preparedness.
Location  Sensors  Communication System  Alert Dissemination  Power Supply
Type  Variables to Measure
Nakhon Si Thammarat, Thailand  STARLFLOW Ultrasonic Doppler sensor  Water level and velocity  GPRS module  Web application. SMS, FAX, email.  Connected to the electrical grid and UPS
Tipping bucket rain gauge  Amount of rain
Florida, United States  Ultrasonic sensor WL700  Water level  Wireless unit (IEEE 802.15)  Online access to raw and predicted data, video information  Photovoltaic system
Redeye Z205 Cameras  Ethernet
Barranquilla, Colombia  Humidity sensor  Atmospheric variables  ZigBee (IEEE 802.15)  Web and mobile application  Photovoltaic system
Temperature sensor
Atmospheric pressure
Manila, Philippines  Pressure sensor  Water level  GPRS module  Web application  Photovoltaic system
Tipping bucket rain gauge  Amount of rain
Mayagüez, Puerto Rico  Weather radar  Radar reflectivity and amount of rain  Parabolic antenna (IEEE 802.15)  Web application  Photovoltaic system
Barcelona, Spain  Weather radar  Radar reflectivity and amount of rain  Web application, SMS, E-mail
In addition to the ultrasonic sensors, the deployment of a GPRS module facilitates immediate data transmission to a web
application, allowing stakeholders to access information remotely and promptly. This digital infrastructure supports various
communication methods, including SMS, FAX, and email notifications, ensuring that relevant parties are kept informed of
changing water conditions. The table presented herein outlines the specific types of data collected and the variables to measure,
illustrating the comprehensive approach taken in Nakhon Si Thammarat to enhance water resource management through
technology.
